topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red ink on the paper seemed to bleed into my vision. I stared at the 'D-'. My hands, usually steady enough to ace any exam, trembled slightly as I traced the low mark with a fingertip. A heavy, dull ache settled in my chest, making it hard to draw a full breath. I reread the feedback, the critiques sharp and unforgiving, each sentence landing like a small stone in my stomach. I wanted to crumple the paper, to throw it across the room, but my fingers remained frozen above the cheap printer. The fluorescent lights of the library hummed with an oppressive, mocking sound. I slowly pushed my chair back, the scrape of the metal legs echoing loudly in the quiet study area. I just wanted to disappear.

[story 2]

I kept replaying the lecture in my head, searching for the moment I’d misunderstood the core concept. Nothing. It felt like my brain had simply shut down during the assessment. When the results finally appeared on the screen, a strange hollowness expanded in my core. My shoulders slumped forward, a physical weight pressing down on my neck. I picked up my pencil, but the graphite felt foreign, clumsy in my grip. I chewed on the end of it, the dry taste filling my mouth. "This isn't right," I mumbled to the empty room, the words barely audible. I looked out the window at the grey afternoon sky, feeling utterly adrift, as if the ground beneath me had dissolved.

[story 3]

The notification popped up, stark and undeniable: 68%. It was a number that felt disproportionately heavy, a monument to wasted effort. I leaned my forehead against the cool surface of my desk, closing my eyes tightly. A slow, involuntary shudder ran through my frame. It wasn't anger; it was just a profound lack of energy, a draining sensation that left me feeling brittle. I opened my eyes and watched dust motes dance in the single shaft of sunlight cutting across the floor. Every movement felt labored, as if I were wading through thick water. I picked up my backpack, the weight suddenly unbearable, and started walking toward the exit, the sound of my footsteps sounding too loud, too clumsy in the hallway.
